expectations.text: Returns exactly ten non-duplicate issues spanning at least seven different quality domains.
expectations.evidence: Exactly ten findings span save, map input, combat, reward transaction, equipment, story, storage integrity, audio, mobile, and deployment.

expectations.text: Each issue contains reproduction/evidence, player impact, root cause, concrete fix, and pass condition.
expectations.evidence: All findings consistently include reproduction, impact, root cause, repair, pass condition, and old-player protection.

expectations.text: Includes at least one save migration issue and one public cache/deployment issue.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 covers historical save migration; finding 10 covers mixed-version static resources and cache.

expectations.text: Includes at least one strategic-truth issue such as fake routes or weak reward choice, not only UI polish.
expectations.evidence: Map and reward findings focus on double-click/idempotency failures. The response never tests whether route branches constrain strategy or whether reward candidates create meaningful construction choices.

expectations.text: Explains compatibility protections for active players and stable links.
expectations.evidence: It gives extensive old-save/static-resource protection, but does not require preserving a canonical direct-play URL or distinguish the stable play link from repository/deployment destinations.

expectations.text: Does not treat HTTP 200 or syntax success as proof of visual/gameplay correctness.
expectations.evidence: The introduction explicitly states that link accessibility does not prove functional correctness and the checks remain behavior-driven.

uncertainties: Hypothesis-driven because source and network inspection were intentionally prohibited.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong baseline on transaction safety, but weaker on strategic truth and stable public-entry compatibility.
